title: UK parliamentary group launches probe into crypto sector banking (3 minute read)
content: The UK's Crypto and Digital Assets All-Party Parliamentary Group launched an inquiry into banking access difficulties faced by crypto businesses, inviting written evidence through August 31 and comparing approaches in the US, Hong Kong, Australia, and the EU. The probe follows the UK's new crypto regulatory framework, set to take effect on October 25, 2027, and coincides with Labour's Andy Burnham taking office as prime minister after scrapping the previous government's digital ID scheme.

title: Robinhood Chain tops $430 million in TVL (4 minute read)
content: Robinhood Chain has drawn $431 million in TVL, nearly $400 million in stablecoin market cap, and over 250,000 daily active users in under three weeks, processing roughly 6 million transactions daily, per FalconX. Memecoins still account for more than 80% of its $9 billion in cumulative DEX volume, but FalconX's Martin Gaspar argues tokenized real-world assets (currently just $14 million versus $851 million for Ondo and $481 million for xStocks) represent the chain's real long-term differentiator given Robinhood's ~28 million-user distribution advantage.

title: New anti-rug mechanics for Bankr token launches (2 minute read)
content: Every new Bankr launch now permanently locks 0.285% of each swap into the liquidity pool with no withdraw function for anyone, and caps any wallet at 2% of supply for the first 24 hours to curb whale dominance, following an earlier curve redesign that lifted peak launch valuations from $8M to $53M on replayed trading data.

title: Sablier Labs Enters Maintenance Mode, Halts Development (4 minute read)
content: Sablier Labs, a token-streaming and vesting infrastructure provider with 837,000+ transactions and 547,000+ vesting plans deployed across 30+ EVM chains and Solana, has halted active development and entered maintenance mode through June 2028, blocking new vesting streams with end dates beyond that cutoff. Co-founder Paul Berg attributed the shutdown to customers postponing token launches amid Q1 2026 market deterioration and AI-assisted coding enabling competitors to replicate the protocol at lower cost. Sablier accelerated its BSL 1.1 to GPL license conversion from the originally planned July 2029 date, granting the community immediate rights to fork, modify, and deploy the contracts. Existing onchain streams and vesting plans remain operational, as the smart contracts run without dependency on Sablier's infrastructure.

title: Top 10 Must-Try Agent Services – Pay-Per-Use via x402 (3 minute read)
content: A curated two-part thread catalogues 20 pay-per-call agent services built on x402, spanning on-chain wallet intelligence, real-world commerce execution, financial research, and media access. Services range from Arkham's per-request blockchain analytics and cited property reports to Resy restaurant bookings, USDC-funded prepaid debit cards, and AI/deepfake detection endpoints. Infrastructure tools are also represented, including normalized LLM pricing feeds for 2,000+ models, a URL-to-cited-knowledge converter, and a bounty-hunting layer where agents fulfill tasks to earn.

title: The Machiavellian Case for Decentralized Networks (10 minute read)
content: Decentralization is the only pragmatic defense against corporate capture: any settlement network reaching scale will attract incumbents whose business models depend on controlling payment rails, following the same pattern as Visa and Mastercard's consolidation of card networks and Google's pivot to advertising. Permissioned enterprise blockchains and L2s with concentrated validators require trusting specific institutions to remain neutral, a condition that fails when profit incentives and network control converge. False decentralization is inferior to both real TradFi efficiency and genuine censorship-resistance, making it a delay tactic for incumbents rather than a durable infrastructure alternative. Permissionless L1s like Ethereum, despite their capture-resistance costs, represent the Nash equilibrium endpoint as assets flow toward the most neutral settlement infrastructure.

title: Sovereign Space: When Values Need Architecture (5 minute read)
content: Sovereignty over private information requires cryptographic enforcement rather than institutional policy, framing the gap between declaring and building sovereign space as the central engineering problem of the current moment. The proposed BeTrueCore architecture combines zero-knowledge proofs with MACI encryption for vote protection, Lit Protocol time-locks to prevent bandwagon effects, MPC-based biometric identity without centralized storage, and Celestia plus Ethereum for immutable audit trails. The system's core design principle removes observers from the measurement process entirely, using cryptographically-enforced blind sessions rather than improving signal fidelity. The work remains pre-MVP theoretical architecture, and the author invites community critique on Byzantine fault assumptions and coordinator-layer vulnerabilities.

title: Bank of Korea Scales Up CBDC Pilot With Half a Million Users (4 minute read)
content: The Bank of Korea is launching Phase 2 of its CBDC pilot in September, scaling from 81,000 wallets across seven banks to a 500,000-user cap with nine participating institutions and an open-ended timeline. Phase 1 (April-June 2025) processed 114,880 transactions across 12,000 merchants, though only 42% of wallet holders actively spent tokens despite 30-35 billion won in bank infrastructure investment. Phase 2 adds biometric approvals, P2P transfers, recurring payments, and interest payments, and will test programmable tokens for government subsidy disbursements with built-in spending restrictions. On a two-tier structure where the Bank of Korea issues wholesale CBDC to commercial banks that distribute deposit tokens to consumers, the programmable token capability has drawn criticism because every transaction creates logged records accessible to central authorities.

title: Public Hyperliquid Treasury Firm Enters 500k HYPE Bond Agreement (3 minute read)
content: Hyperion DeFi (HYPD), a Nasdaq-listed HYPE token treasury company, signed a HYPE Asset Use Service agreement with Skew Technologies, deploying 500,000 staked HYPE (roughly $33.59M) in exchange for equity in Skew and a share of its market listing revenue.

title: NVIDIA/USDG becomes largest Robinhood Chain RWA pool (1 minute read)
content: The NVDA/USDG Uniswap v4 pool on Robinhood Chain is now the largest RWA pool on the network.

title: Base to launch 1:1-backed tokenized equities (1 minute read)
content: Pollak said Base and Coinbase are close to launching tokenized equities backed 1:1 by real shares, contrasting with Robinhood's derivative-based stock tokens, arguing the combination will scale better on trust, capital efficiency, and institutional acceptance.

title: Ramp opens up its internal LLM cost-routing tool (2 minute read)
content: Ramp is opening its internal AI router, which cut its own AI costs 30% and now handles 2.75 trillion tokens a month, to outside users for free as an OpenAI-compatible endpoint that auto-routes requests to the cheapest model clearing a quality bar.

title: Strategy sells $263.5 million in MSTR shares, buys no bitcoin (3 minute read)
content: Strategy sold 2.7 million MSTR shares for roughly $263.5 million between July 13 and July 19 without buying or selling any bitcoin, pushing its USD reserve up $225 million to $3.225 billion. The firm's holdings remain at 843,775 BTC (~$54.7 billion, about 4% of bitcoin's supply cap) bought at an average price of $75,476, carrying roughly $9 billion in paper losses. CEO Phong Le said Strategy would only reassess debt-related risk if bitcoin fell to $8,000-$10,000, while MSTR stock closed the week down 4% at $94.85, off 38.6% year-to-date.

title: Amazon's Largest Japan Delivery Partner to Pay Drivers in Stablecoin (2 minute read)
content: AZ-COM Maruwa, Amazon's largest delivery partner in Japan, will pay 2,300 carriers and independent contractors in JPYC, a yen-backed stablecoin, in what the companies describe as Japan's first large-scale corporate stablecoin payroll deployment. The rollout reflects Japan's amended Payment Services Act, which since 2023 has permitted licensed entities to issue yen-pegged tokens, giving JPYC a regulatory footing absent in most other major economies. The partnership moves stablecoin payment rails into recurring enterprise payroll at logistics scale, a use case distinct from the crypto-native trading and DeFi activity that has dominated prior stablecoin volume metrics.

title: HIP-4 to support permissionless deployment for outcome markets (3 minute read)
content: Hyperliquid will roll out permissionless deployment for HIP-4 outcome markets, launching on testnet first, letting deployers instantiate validator-approved templates rather than requiring validators to create markets directly. Deployers must stake 500,000 HYPE, locked for six months, with stakes subject to slashing for poorly defined markets, bad settlement, or missed settlement deadlines, and can charge fees up to 50%. Each deployer starts with an allocation for 100 outcomes. HIP-4 launched in May and drew about $100 million in volume in its first month.

title: Base Native Account Abstraction (5 minute read)
content: EIP-8130 is a protocol-native account abstraction standard developed with Optimism, Coinbase, and WalletConnect set to ship with the Cobalt hard fork in September and extend to all OP Stack chains later this year. By embedding AA at the protocol layer rather than running it as an ERC-4337 overlay, EIP-8130 cuts USDC transfer gas from 125,000 to 46,000 gas (63%) and calldata from 1,090 to 180 bytes (83%), while delivering batch calls, gas sponsorship, session keys, sub-accounts, cross-chain portability, and quantum-ready key rotation as native protocol features. Standard EOA accounts gain sponsorship and batching without code changes. Existing ERC-4337 smart accounts require a one-time migration to access the lower cost structure. Developers can test EIP-8130 on Base Vibenet, an ephemeral devnet available ahead of the Cobalt activation.

title: Inference Farming: The Next Yield Farming Wave (2 minute read)
content: "Inference farming" may be the successor to perps yield farming, as decentralized AI inference platforms will accrue revenues to token holders the way Hyperliquid channeled trading fees to HLP/HYPE participants. Open-source models like Kimi K3 reaching frontier performance at reduced cost are the catalyst, with decentralized inference networks able to price 30%-plus below centralized providers as a result. Farmers participate by renting GPUs and contributing compute to these networks. Early project picks named include Dolphin AI, Bittensor subnet 53 (Engy), and AntSeed AI. A quoted thread identifies settlement standardization, reference pricing, and compute derivatives (futures and forwards) as the largest unaddressed gaps across the inference capital markets stack.

title: Is there anything left to build in crypto? (7 minute read)
content: Crypto's foundational infrastructure problems are solved, with L1s, L2s, DeFi, and stablecoins now functioning as mature settlement rails, and the next opportunity lies in building financial infrastructure for autonomous machine actors rather than humans. The firm's "machine economy" thesis holds that AI agents, warehouse robots, and automated research systems require open, programmable, permissionless payment rails that legacy financial infrastructure was not designed to provide. Emerging agent payment standards including x402, MPP, and AP2 are maturing alongside this demand. Coinbase, Robinhood, and Binance have each shipped agentic trading infrastructure in recent months.

title: Saylor urges Bitcoin to reject BIP-110 in 110-point essay (4 minute read)
content: Michael Saylor published a 110-point essay, "110 Reasons BIP 110 Is a Bad Idea," arguing consensus rules shouldn't judge the purpose of valid, fee-paying transactions, drawing over 840,000 views. BIP-110 is a temporary one-year soft fork restricting data-heavy transactions, built on Bitcoin Knots software from backer Luke Dashjr, with a mandatory signaling window opening around August 7. Miner support currently sits at just 0.86%, far below the 55% threshold needed for early lock-in. If signaling stays low, BIP-110-enforcing nodes risk splitting onto a minority chain when the rules take effect around September 1.

title: Airbnb 🤝 Ethereum (5 minute read)
content: Robinhood and Coinbase already run significant core operations on Ethereum L2s, and this represents the opening move of a broad corporate onchain adoption pattern that platforms like Airbnb should follow. The proposed Airbnb blueprint includes a proprietary Ethereum L2, a platform stablecoin for host and guest payments and deposits, and tokenized ABNB equity natively integrated into host and guest rewards, making the stock function as a dual-purpose utility token. A cross-corporate onchain reputation network would feed underwriting for adjacent lending and mortgage products, with host transaction history serving as a proxy for borrower creditworthiness. Corporate crypto/onchain functions are the next standard internal department, comparable to IT or Legal, with Ethereum L2s and RWA infrastructure as the dominant infrastructure layer and ETH as a primary demand beneficiary.

title: 18 tips for neobank founders (2 minute read)
content: Distribution over features, compliance as a core requirement, banking businesses over consumers, and building multiple revenue streams beyond interchange from day one are some of the many ways to quickly, effectively, and sustainably scale a neobank.

title: Andrew Cuomo joins OKX board (1 minute read)
content: Former New York Governor Andrew Cuomo, who has advised OKX on US regulatory strategy since 2023, joined the exchange's board to support its US expansion and will co-chair the OKX-ICE joint venture.

title: Onchain businesses hitting $200M+ revenue milestones (1 minute read)
content: 14 onchain businesses now generate $200M+ in two-year revenue, up from almost none in 2021.

title: Bitcoin devs propose BIP-361 for quantum resistance (2 minute read)
content: A new proposal would block BTC transfers to quantum-vulnerable addresses, phase out legacy signature schemes over five years, and explore a recovery mechanism for users who don't migrate funds in time.

title: Bank of America taps new leaders for crypto, AI and traditional finance (3 minute read)
content: Bank of America named Sonali Theisen head of its global digital assets platform, adding oversight of stablecoins, tokenized deposits, custody, and crypto trade settlement to her existing role leading electronic trading across fixed income, currencies and commodities. Kevin Milsom was tapped as head of AI transformation to drive AI adoption across the bank's global markets platforms. The moves follow similar buildouts at Vanguard, Morgan Stanley, and xAI as Wall Street institutions formalize crypto and AI leadership.

title: US regulators miss GENIUS Act's one-year deadline for stablecoin rules (4 minute read)
content: The OCC, FDIC, NCUA, and Treasury reached the GENIUS Act's July 18 rulemaking deadline without issuing final implementing regulations, leaving several major proposals unfinished. Comments on a joint customer identification rule stay open until August 21, and an FDIC anti-money laundering proposal runs through August 4. The delay doesn't push back the law's January 18 effective date, compressing the window for issuers to prepare. Unresolved issues include BlackRock's push to drop a proposed 20% cap on tokenized reserve assets and senators' concerns over preserving state regulatory authority.

title: Visa Launches Stablecoin Platform for 200M+ Merchants (3 minute read)
content: Visa has launched the Visa Stablecoin Platform, integrating stablecoin transactions into the existing payment and treasury workflows of its 15,000 financial institution partners and 200+ million merchant network without requiring new payment infrastructure. The platform debuts with Open USD (OUSD), a dollar-backed stablecoin issued by the Open Standard consortium, co-founded by Visa, Mastercard, and American Express, with USDC from Circle and USDG from Paxos also supported at launch. Visa already settles about $15 trillion in payments per year and processes several billion dollars in stablecoin settlements through existing products, with the new platform consolidating those capabilities under a single interface offering instant settlement and blockchain-based transaction records.

title: Privacy Guardians 2.0 (4 minute read)
content: Privacy Guardians is a decentralized payment privacy system built to counter centralized alternatives that expose transaction data and retain fund-freezing authority. The architecture combines private payment infrastructure, insurance frameworks, honeypot claiming mechanisms, exchange rate management, liquidity pool structures, and metadata handling protocols to maximize on-chain privacy without custodial intermediaries. There are open gaps in cryptographic soundness, mechanism elegance, incentive alignment, and capital efficiency, and invited community refinement. The release targets a persistent problem in blockchain payments where privacy-preserving alternatives often reintroduce centralized chokepoints.

title: The ANSEM Thesis (7 minute read)
content: Ansem launched $ANSEM as an explicit attempt to merge memecoin speculation with creator economy monetization, citing his 1.1M followers, an X podcast averaging 10M views per episode, and $1.4M in pump.fun creator fees generated in two weeks as the platform foundation for the token's value proposition. Ansem holds ~58% of total supply and plans milestone-gated staggered airdrops to active community contributors, while targeting DeFi protocol partnerships structured around Hyperliquid's incentive alignment model and Bonk's attention conversion playbook. The token thesis references $TRUMP's $80B and $DOGE's $88B peak caps as proof-of-concept predecessors, differentiating $ANSEM's structure from failed predecessors such as Friend.Tech, Time.fun, and celebrity coins. Ansem frames the tokenized creator economy as the next distinct crypto vertical after RWAs, projecting the broader creator economy will reach $2T by 2035 and positioning creator tokens as the most capital-efficient go-to-market layer for that sector.

title: The time for Clarity is here: The next-generation Telecoms Act (5 minute read)
content: Former House Financial Services Committee Chair Patrick McHenry, in a Fortune op-ed shared by a16z crypto, positions the Clarity Act as the first proactive major US financial legislation since Gramm-Leach-Bliley, comparing its scope to the Telecommunications Act of 1996 in establishing forward-looking consumer safeguards and market structure for a nascent technology. The bill, advancing through Congress with bipartisan support across multiple committees, would create consumer and investor protections alongside enforcement tools for law enforcement, building on the GENIUS Act stablecoin framework that has already cleared both chambers. McHenry contends that defined regulatory boundaries accelerate entrepreneurial activity rather than suppress it, pointing to internet-era startup formation as precedent, and warns that other jurisdictions courting crypto capital with clearer rules pose a direct competitive threat to US market leadership.

title: Request for Builders: Funding the Future of Global Finance (8 minute read)
content: Base's Ecosystem Fund published a Request for Builders spanning six high-conviction verticals, directing pre-seed and seed capital toward startups building financial infrastructure on the L2. Priority areas include tokenized alternative yield-bearing assets (invoice financing, REITs, and private credit funds), DeFi-accessible securities-backed credit lines built on tokenized brokerage portfolios, and stablecoin distribution networks targeting de-facto dollarized markets including Argentina, Venezuela, and Lebanon. The credit vertical calls for zkTLS-based underwriting that combines off-chain salary and bank account data with onchain credit history, enabling unsecured consumer lending without centralized data brokers. An agentic commerce vertical identifies AI-driven shopping, booking, and ticketing agents as stablecoin volume drivers, with x402 named as the intended payment protocol for machine-to-merchant transactions.

title: Dune research finds 85% of concentrated DeFi liquidity is underutilized (4 minute read)
content: A Dune Analytics study commissioned by 1inch found that 85% of concentrated liquidity across Uniswap v3/v4, PancakeSwap v3, and Aerodrome Slipstream operates below optimal efficiency, with about $1.6 billion of $1.84 billion in average weekly tracked liquidity sitting idle across 200 top-performing pools. LPs forgo about $150 million per year in fee income, led by Uniswap at roughly $116 million, derived from in-range capital earning approximately $0.35 per dollar deployed per year. Positions above $1 million account for 47% of idle capital (roughly $260 million), and individual wallet holders rather than bots or contracts control the majority of dormant positions across major chains. Uniswap v4 showing 30.5% out-of-range capital alongside 36.7% of all positions untouched for 90-plus days indicates that structural inefficiency in concentrated liquidity persists beyond protocol-level redesigns.

title: Hayden Adams: Robinhood RWA pools gaining traction on Uniswap (1 minute read)
content: Tokenized RWA pools on Robinhood Chain are growing quickly on Uniswap even as meme volumes still lead.

title: Bubblemaps: Robinhood memecoin traders mostly losing money (1 minute read)
content: Of 164,538 traders across the top 50 memecoins on Robinhood, 63% lost money while 37% made money.

title: Cobie: Coinbase Has Long Been Distant From Crypto-Native Users (1 minute read)
content: Cobie, who took over Coinbase's Base App and trading products just days ago, publicly acknowledged that both Coinbase and Base have severely eroded crypto-native user trust through a series of avoidable mistakes.

title: Token Terminal: Robinhood Chain leads in stock token holder growth (1 minute read)
content: Robinhood Chain added roughly 190,000 stock token holders over the past 30 days, more than any other blockchain, and now accounts for about 27% of all onchain stock token holders.

title: Bitcoin Climbs to $65K on CPI Miss as Cohorts Sell Into Rally (3 minute read)
content: Bitcoin climbed to ~$65,000 after June CPI printed 3.5% YoY against a 3.8% consensus with core at 2.6%, pulling the dollar index down 0.5% to 100.48. On-chain data shows two cohorts selling into the move: long-term holders (5+ months) who bought near 2025 highs are realizing losses at tighter margins than those available below $60,000, while short-term holders who accumulated near recent lows are distributing at rates above $4 million daily, matching the pace of May's bounce toward $82,000. Bitget's Ryan Lee flagged the headline as potentially stale, noting the 3.5% print was driven by a 10% gasoline drop that had already reversed with oil back at one-month highs. Wintermute's Jasper De Maere added that a single soft CPI print against active military escalation does not constitute a durable macro regime shift.

title: Strategy Secure Until $8K BTC, CEO Says (3 minute read)
content: Strategy CEO Phong Le stated the company's balance sheet remains secure unless Bitcoin falls to the $8,000-$10,000 range, an ~85% drop from current prices near $64,500, at which point debt-related risks would require reassessment. Two structural stress signals cut against that framing: preferred stock STRC has dropped below its $100 par value to under $75, restricting new share issuance for Bitcoin purchases, and the company's mNAV has compressed to 1.02, erasing the premium that powered its equity-to-BTC accumulation flywheel. MSTR shares fell close to 6% on the day and are down 78% over the past year. Le's emphasis on building USD liquidity reserves marks a tactical shift from the company's prior posture of continuous BTC accumulation funded through capital markets.

title: Arc deleted the mempool, and few noticed (6 minute read)
content: Circle's May arXiv paper describes Arc's Multi-Proposer Protocol (AMP), which eliminates the public mempool that exposes pending transactions to front-running bots and lets block builders reorder trades for profit. Instead, dedicated proposers hand transactions directly to validators under bounded inclusion guarantees. Built on the Malachite BFT consensus engine, Arc's testnet has processed over 244 million transactions since October 2025, with roughly 3,000 TPS and sub-350-millisecond finality. It charges gas in USDC rather than a volatile token and is expected to reach mainnet this summer with quantum-secure signatures from launch.

title: Fynd now reads Flashblocks on Base (4 minute read)
content: Base's Flashblock architecture produces new block state every 200ms, but most DEX aggregators rely on RPC calls that take 250ms+ per fetch, leaving routers operating on state 10-50 Flashblocks behind and top aggregators potentially 15 seconds stale across hundreds of thousands of pools. PropAMMs exploit this gap by posting competitive prices in the final Flashblock of block N, then widening spreads at the start of block N+1 when trades settle. 0x analysis across 461 blocks found this pattern occurs 97.4% of the time, costing traders 5-10 bps per affected swap. Fynd, PropellerHeads' locally deployed router, streams Flashblock deltas into in-memory state so every quote reflects current rather than prior-block pricing, leaving propAMMs unable to predict which Flashblock state Fynd routes against and wrong on 9 of 10 Flashblocks. The local deployment also removes the ~500ms round-trip overhead of external aggregator APIs, a latency edge CEX-DEX arbitrage teams require when building hundreds of quotes per pair per block.

title: Dear Robinhood, please do not build a memecoin chain (4 minute read)
content: Robinhood Chain has posted strong early traction, including 300K+ daily active addresses, $1B+ in daily spot DEX volume (second among all chains), $40M in annualized fees, and $300M+ in TVL anchored by Morpho, Ethena, and Uniswap. Memecoin trading accounts for the majority of that DEX volume, threatening the chain's long-term viability. Memecoins launched on Base in early 2024 are down 90% this year and 99% from peak, destroying retail trust and reinforcing Robinhood's association with the GME-era speculative cycle rather than credible financial infrastructure. The preferred path redirects liquidity toward tokenized stocks, RWAs, and prediction markets. The bull case holds that 100M MAUs by 2030 at $100 ARPU produces $10B in consumer revenue, topping the current analyst FY29 consensus of $8.78B.

title: A cold-storage multisig setup for serious crypto holdings (4 minute read)
content: Responding to ZachXBT's claim that hardware wallets are unsafe, this thread outlines a 2-of-3 Safe multisig setup using three independent signer devices (a locked-down dedicated iPhone, a separate phone or hardware wallet, and an offline backup device), each requiring approval from two of three before funds move. The thread recommends stripping the dedicated signing device of SIM, email, and social apps, storing recovery phrases offline on paper or steel, testing the full setup with a small transaction first, and keeping only spending-money amounts in a separate everyday wallet.

title: Deep dive: privacy tokens are the only crypto sector up this cycle (3 minute read)
content: Privacy tokens climbed 127.3% over the past year, the best-performing crypto sector, despite 73 exchanges delisting privacy tokens in 2025. This post traces the trend to Bitcoin's public ledger breaking fungibility by making coin history and wallet identities traceable. It explains how zero-knowledge proofs let networks verify a transaction is valid without revealing sender, receiver, or amount, and covers the compliance divide separating projects like Monero and Zcash as governments push conflicting rules on private digital money.

title: Visa says stablecoins will power micro-commerce in AI agentic economy (4 minute read)
content: AI agentic commerce will split into macro-commerce (consumer-sized purchases like travel booking, suited to card rails) and micro-commerce (frequent sub-dollar payments between software systems, better served by low-cost stablecoin settlement). Visa expects a hybrid model where card-native protocols like its own Visa Intelligent Commerce add stablecoin support while crypto-native protocols adopt traditional trust infrastructure, though it acknowledges that existing legal frameworks and chargeback systems weren't designed for autonomous agents transacting without direct human judgment.

title: US Senate unanimously opposes clemency for Sam Bankman-Fried (1 minute read)
content: The US Senate passed a nonbinding resolution by unanimous consent stating SBF should not receive a presidential pardon, sentence commutation, or any other federal clemency, following his efforts to seek clemency and Trump's earlier pardons of Binance founder Changpeng Zhao and Silk Road founder Ross Ulbricht.

title: DefiLlama's MiCA dashboard now tracks onramp/offramp costs (1 minute read)
content: DefiLlama added onramp and offramp cost tracking to its MiCA dashboard, breaking down the full cost of converting euros to bitcoin and back, including SEPA transfer fees, taker fees, slippage, and network withdrawal fees.

title: Stanford study finds manipulation on Polymarket's 5-min BTC markets (2 minute read)
content: A study by Stanford University and Singapore Management University researchers found 821 suspected manipulators exploited Polymarket's 5-minute Bitcoin markets, pocketing $8.2 million by placing concentrated trades in the final seconds before settlement to briefly move Bitcoin's price before it reversed.

title: Coinbase Institute: stablecoins pose no threat to small bank deposits (2 minute read)
content: A new Coinbase Institute paper finds no meaningful link between stablecoin growth and bank deposit levels, noting USDC grew 4.6% over the past six months while total demand deposits grew 4.5% in parallel.

title: Japan passes key bill recognizing crypto as financial product (4 minute read)
content: Japan's parliament passed amendments to the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act reclassifying crypto as a financial product similar to stocks and bonds rather than a payment method, adding insider-trading prohibitions, mandatory issuer disclosures, and prison terms up to ten years for unregistered operations. The reform sets up separate crypto taxation at an effective ~20% rate (down from up to 55%) with three-year loss carry-forwards, taking effect around January 2028, and lays groundwork for domestic spot crypto ETFs, which the Japan Exchange Group is eyeing as early as 2027.

title: Czech Republic moves to block Polymarket over unlicensed gambling (3 minute read)
content: The Czech Ministry of Finance added Polymarket to its list of unauthorized internet games, requiring ISPs to block the platform within 15 days, joining Germany, Belgium, Romania, Switzerland, Poland, Greece, Cyprus, Portugal, Spain, Ukraine and Brazil in restricting access. The crackdown coincides with US regulatory pressure, including a Polymarket-affiliated entity's pending NFA application for regulated margin trading and a Senate call for a CFTC investigation into alleged fake betting promotions. Despite the headwinds, combined monthly volume across Kalshi, Polymarket and Polymarket US rose 75% in June to $44.8 billion amid World Cup activity.

title: EthSystems Launches to Build Confidential Infrastructure for Institutional Ethereum (3 minute read)
content: EthSystems launched as an independent company building modular confidentiality infrastructure for institutional Ethereum, spinning out from a year operating as the Ethereum Foundation's Institutional Privacy Task Force (IPTF). During that period, the team engaged hundreds of central banks, tier-one banks, regulators, and asset managers while shipping open-source deliverables including private bonds, confidential stablecoin transfers, private cross-chain settlement, and the Ethereum Privacy Map. Institutional adoption of Ethereum as commercial infrastructure for stablecoins, tokenized assets, and settlement is blocked by the absence of built-in privacy tooling, since banks running real financial flows cannot operate without transaction-level confidentiality on a public ledger. Backed by Ethereum-aligned investors, EthSystems frames the institutional privacy build-out as a decade-long transition.

title: Visa, Mastercard, and Ripple Back x402 (4 minute read)
content: The Linux Foundation launched the x402 Foundation with 40 member companies, including Visa, Mastercard, Ripple, American Express, Stripe, Google, AWS, and the Solana and Stellar foundations, formalizing a stablecoin payment standard Coinbase originally built. The protocol activates HTTP's dormant 402 "Payment Required" response code, enabling direct USDC transfers between clients and servers without accounts or prior relationships, settling in seconds. Over the past 30 days, the network processed 75 million transactions totaling $24 million at an average of 32 cents per payment, with approximately 94,000 buyers and 22,000 sellers transacting at 29 TPS. The design targets autonomous AI agents, which cannot open bank accounts or sign traditional contracts but can execute blockchain transactions, making x402 suited for agent-to-agent commerce at transaction sizes card networks cannot serve at a profit.

title: Interviewing Engineers in the AI Era: Lessons from a Year of Rebuilding (7 minute read)
content: Coinbase detailed a year-long overhaul of its engineering interview process after AI-generated code crossed 50% of merged commits in Q4 2025, up from 5.7% in Q1 2025. The company found its old interviews produced false positives (candidates who memorized patterns) and false negatives (candidates with strong judgment but less recall), leading to a three-phase rebuild spanning frontend, backend, and company-wide rollout that now evaluates candidates on AI usage, application judgment, and understanding of AI's limits through live repo-based debugging and behavioral rounds, with a system-design-with-AI round still in early testing.

title: TradFi doesn't want DeFi, it wants blockchain (7 minute read)
content: Institutions are selectively adopting blockchain technology, such as atomic settlement, shared ledgers, and programmable money, while discarding DeFi's core properties of open access, pseudonymity, and permissionless execution, producing a new category the firm calls "programmable financial infrastructure." JPMorgan's permissioned deposit chain, BlackRock and Franklin Templeton's tokenized money market funds, and Circle's Arc are examples of institutions using blockchain plumbing without embracing decentralization. Builders should choose clearly between serving institutional buyers or open crypto-native networks rather than pursuing both, since the two require fundamentally different products, sales cycles, and success metrics.

title: Investing in Autonomy: Ten Hypotheses (4 minute read)
content: Variant Fund published 10 working hypotheses on agentic AI as a public scorecard, with plans to update their views as the domain evolves. They expect frontier AI access to grow more permissioned over time, spurring demand for open, permissionless alternatives, while also projecting that agents will surpass humans as the dominant source of internet traffic, requiring products designed for both audiences. On value accrual, orchestration harnesses owning the user experience will prove more durable than individual models as models commoditize, and that whoever defines the coordination layer for multiplayer agentic systems holds the primary point of leverage. Variant is actively deploying capital against all 10 theses.

title: Agentic Commerce for Dummies (6 minute read)
content: Two competing agent-native payment protocols, x402 (Coinbase) and MPP (Stripe/Tempo), enable AI agents to pay for API calls per-request without accounts or subscriptions, addressing the core infrastructure gap blocking agentic commerce. Card fee structures (~$0.30 + 2.9% per transaction) render agent microtransactions unviable, which is why both protocols settle in stablecoins on Base, Solana, and Tempo where fees approach zero, enabling cent-level per-use pricing. Discovery layers x402scan and mppscan index participating merchants and live transaction volume, giving agents a directory to find and transact with services on demand. Open USD, backed by Mastercard, Visa, Coinbase, Stripe, and BlackRock among 140+ partners, launched as confirmation that major card networks and asset managers are committing capital to stablecoin payment rails.

title: Bitmine generated $45.7M from ETH staking last quarter, 98% of revenue (1 minute read)
content: Bitmine Immersion Technologies' 10-Q filing shows $45.7 million in Ethereum staking and validation revenue for the quarter ended May 31, up from $2 million a year earlier.

title: Robinhood Chain Earns $816K, Ethereum Gets $1,538 (2 minute read)
content: Robinhood Chain has generated ~$816K in gross revenue since inception, but Ethereum captured only $1,538 (0.15%) of that sum.

title: South Korea to bring crypto under new state asset management law (2 minute read)
content: South Korea's Ministry of Finance and Economy will establish a "National Asset Basic Act" to replace the 1950-era State Property Act, expanding state asset management to cover new classes including intellectual property and virtual assets.

title: Binance pursues "super app" strategy beyond trading (1 minute read)
content: Binance's Shunyet Jan told CoinDesk the exchange is expanding into payments and broader financial services as stablecoin usage grows beyond trading, echoing a similar "super app" ambition previously expressed by Coinbase.

title: Strategy sells $467M in MSTR shares, leaves 800k BTC stack untouched (3 minute read)
content: Strategy raised $466.7 million through a 4.8 million Class A share ATM sale between July 6 and July 12, growing its dollar cash reserve from $2.55 billion to $3 billion while leaving its 843,775 BTC treasury untouched at an average cost of $75,476 per coin, approximately 17% above BTC's current spot price of $62,580. The equity raise follows a prior week in which the company sold 3,588 BTC for approximately $216 million to fund preferred stock dividends and debt service, signaling a deliberate shift toward equity-side capital to protect the BTC stack. Strategy retains $23.8 billion in ATM capacity, including a $21 billion tranche announced March 23, and initiated semi-monthly STRC preferred dividends with the first payment scheduled July 15. MSTR shares fell approximately 3% to $91.80 ahead of Monday's Nasdaq open alongside a broader BTC pullback of over 2% in 24 hours.

title: Wall Street turns cautious on Circle (4 minute read)
content: Mizuho downgraded Circle to Underperform and cut its price target 41% to $50, while JPMorgan lowered earnings estimates for both Circle and Coinbase, citing pressure on USDC economics from the new Open USD stablecoin consortium (140+ members including Visa, Mastercard, Stripe, BlackRock, and Coinbase) that routes nearly all reserve yield to distributors versus Circle's roughly 38% retention model. JPMorgan pointed to Circle's revised Hyperliquid deal, where Coinbase now takes all reserve income before returning about 90% to the DEX, as a "prisoner's dilemma" pushing Circle and Coinbase to compete on revenue-sharing terms. Bernstein and William Blair remain bullish, keeping Outperform ratings and arguing Circle's liquidity and regulatory lead are hard to replicate.

title: Over $7.2B Migrates From LayerZero to Chainlink CCIP (3 minute read)
content: The $292M Kelp bridge exploit in May 2026 exposed configuration vulnerabilities in LayerZero's OFT standard, triggering a $7.2B migration to Chainlink CCIP, with Mantle's Super Portal (co-developed with Bybit) contributing $2.5B during a July 9-15 window covering MNT transfers from Ethereum and Solana. Kelp ($1.5B), Lombard ($1B+), Solv Protocol ($700M), Virtuals Protocol ($700M), Re ($475M), and Kraken ($330M) are among the largest migrators, spanning DeFi protocols and centralized exchanges. The preference for CCIP centers on the CCT standard's direct pool and setting controls for token issuers, combined with Chainlink's decentralized oracle network as the security layer for cross-chain transfers. The participant mix, crossing protocol-layer DeFi and CeFi, indicates a preference shift among high-TVL cross-chain operators away from OFT toward CCT as the dominant bridging standard for large asset volumes.

title: Japan's SBI to Launch Yen Stablecoin Lending With 3% Yield (3 minute read)
content: SBI VC Trade opens JPYSC lending applications on July 16, offering a 3% annualized yield on 12-week terms, outpacing traditional yen deposit rates of 0.325%-1% per year. The product carries retail risk disclosures: no deposit insurance, no early redemption, and tokens fall outside statutory asset segregation protections. SBI Holdings also formalized a strategic partnership with Solana Foundation, renaming subsidiary SBI R3 Japan to SBI Solana Global with a mandate to build Japanese onchain financial markets and expand stablecoin and tokenized RWA adoption across Asia. The launch follows Japan's April 2026 reclassification of crypto assets as financial instruments, with Prime Minister Takaichi pledging reduced regulatory burdens and increased Web3 funding.

title: Asset-Based Finance is already onchain; tranching is how you scale it (7 minute read)
content: Tokenized asset-based finance (ABF), a ~$6 trillion private market projected by KKR to exceed $9 trillion by 2029, has successfully moved granular receivables pools onchain via platforms like Midas, Figure and Fasanara. However, most tokenized ABF still issues a single undifferentiated risk token rather than a true tranched capital stack. Tranching, splitting a pool into senior and junior claims via a waterfall, is what allows granular pools to reach investment grade. Strata has structured six live markets since October 2025 across Ethena, Midas, and Figure with $2B+ minted, 20,000+ users and nine audits, with senior/junior tranches performing as designed during a stress event on its Saturn USDat market.

title: The Future of RWA - Robinhood Chain (8 minute read)
content: Robinhood Chain launches as an Arbitrum-based L2 built specifically for tokenized real-world assets, arriving with 28 million pre-funded accounts across 38 countries and waived gas fees during the initial period, rather than building infrastructure first and sourcing an audience afterward. CEO Vlad Tenev has stated that tokenization will become the default mechanism for non-US investors to access US equities, and Robinhood's decade of brokerage operations, $HOOD public listing, and active SEC engagement on a tokenized-equity regulatory framework give the chain trust credibility that crypto-native protocols cannot source retroactively. Launch partners span the full stack: Uniswap (AMM), Chainlink (oracles/CCIP), Morpho (lending), Paxos (tokenized asset issuance), LayerZero (interoperability), and TRM Labs (compliance), with Citigroup projecting the broader RWA market at $5.5 trillion by 2030.

title: What Happened to Conferences? (6 minute read)
content: Mega crypto conferences are losing attendee quality through a self-reinforcing cycle: high-signal founders and investors migrate to curated side events and invite-only dinners, main-event composition degrades, and others follow. Crypto talent centralizing around New York and a handful of other cities has also reduced the geographic necessity that conferences originally addressed for an industry with no home city. The decline mirrors crypto's outward turn into the broader economy: stablecoins have penetrated traditional finance faster than forecast, Hyperliquid is shipping oil futures, and TradFi conferences now run stablecoin and prediction-market tracks. The probable endpoint is fewer mega conferences on the current every-eight-weeks treadmill, with the "crypto conference" label fading the way "internet conference" did once every conference became one.

title: The DeFi Hackpocalypse Is a False Alarm (2 minute read)
content: H1 2026 recorded 207 DeFi hack incidents, a record count, yet total losses reached only $972M, less than half of H1 2025 and below the $1B threshold, per Immunefi data. There was a May 2026 prediction that AI-powered attackers would trigger a sector-wide hackpocalypse. The pattern reflects a security bifurcation: large protocols deployed AI-hardening tools and kept losses contained, while smaller protocols and abandonware absorbed the spike in attack volume. Attackers using GLM 5.2, Fable, and GPT 5.6 are finding success against under-resourced targets rather than blue-chip TVL.

title: SEC Crypto Task Force meets Hyperliquid Policy Center and XYZ (1 minute read)
content: SEC Crypto Task Force staff met July 14 with the Hyperliquid Policy Center, XYZ Ltd.

title: EthSystems to build confidential systems for institutional Ethereum (3 minute read)
content: Oskar Thoren is leaving the Ethereum Foundation to launch EthSystems alongside co-founders, building modular privacy tooling for institutional Ethereum adoption.

title: Introducing Receive Policies (1 minute read)
content: Tempo is introducing Receive Policies, a protocol-level primitive that lets accounts restrict which tokens and senders can push assets to them, addressing the baseline blockchain behavior where any address can transfer any token to any recipient without consent.

title: Solo Bitcoin Miner Makes $200,000 Using $150 Equipment (3 minute read)
content: A solo miner running a $150 Bitaxe ASIC mined block 957,382 on Public Pool after eight hours of operation, collecting 3.1382 BTC worth about $200,000 against 1-in-18,000-year odds.

title: President Trump, White House press Senate to advance the Clarity Act (5 minute read)
content: Trump urged the Senate to pass the Clarity Act in tribute to Sen. Lindsey Graham, who died over the weekend, framing passage as a way to prevent China from leading in crypto and AI. White House crypto adviser Patrick Witt also pushed for quick action, while new bill text expected this week reportedly excludes an ethics provision limiting officials' ability to profit from crypto. Sen. Elizabeth Warren called the current text "significant flaws" in a letter to Senate leadership, demanding language barring the president, VP, senior officials, and their families from crypto profits before the bill reaches the floor.

title: UK Outlines tokenized finance roadmap (4 minute read)
content: The UK's Wholesale Digital Markets Champion published a roadmap projecting tokenized wholesale markets could add £33 billion in annual economic output and £14 billion in tax revenue by 2035, provided the country moves quickly. Recommendations include prioritizing first issuance under the Digital Gilt Instrument (DIGIT) pilot by Q1 2027, expanding tokenized collateral use, and building payment rails for settling tokenized assets and stablecoins. The report estimates the global tokenized asset market could reach $88 trillion by 2035, and industry figures from Ripple and Banking Circle welcomed the plan while stressing payment infrastructure must modernize alongside it.

title: Why Is Instant Stablecoin Settlement Rarely Instant? (5 minute read)
content: OpenFX illustrates why "instant" stablecoin settlement often isn't, using a narrative comparison of two $50 million Mexico-to-Dubai transfers: one completes in 18 minutes because the provider has direct SPEI access, automated dual-regulator compliance, and pre-funded AED liquidity, while the other takes roughly 36 hours due to manual batch processing, a stalled compliance review awaiting London sign-off, thin dirham liquidity forcing a worse price, and missing the UAEFTS settlement window. The blockchain leg of a transaction (roughly five seconds) is rarely the bottleneck. The real determinant of speed and cost is the surrounding operational and compliance infrastructure that most "instant" claims ignore.

title: The Zero-Human Company: 281 AI Agent Customers on x402 (3 minute read)
content: The Zero-Human Company, an experiment running on the x402 protocol, reports 281 paying AI-agent customers and about 400 products in inventory, earning close to $680 per week by filling supply gaps in machine-to-machine marketplaces. Grok scans x402 platforms to detect demand concentrations and directs the company to list products where agent demand is highest, exploiting a 5:1 buyer-to-seller imbalance across the ecosystem. X402 allows AI agents to execute micropayments for services without human approval at any transaction step. The lopsided ratio indicates that autonomous agent buyers are already active and spending, while the seller side has not yet scaled to match demand.

title: Real Businesses Make the Ethereum Decision (8 minute read)
content: The crypto industry's next growth phase will be driven by cash businesses rather than token-sale projects, and those businesses are choosing Ethereum's L1+L2 stack. Robinhood Chain uses Ethereum blobs for data availability, ETH as its native gas token, and a canonical Ethereum bridge. Coinbase made the same structural decision in building Base as an L2. The L1+L2 model gives enterprises a credibly neutral settlement hub (L1) paired with a competitive market of customizable execution environments (L2s), while independent L1s require bootstrapping their own security budgets, bridge trust, and liquidity from scratch. As cash-generating businesses displace grant-seeking token projects in the buyer mix, Ethereum's structural advantages become more decisive and ETH's monetary premium strengthens.

title: The Real Liquidity Test for Equity Perps (5 minute read)
content: Equity perpetuals face an execution quality problem: thin order books force leveraged positions through multiple price levels, degrading average fill price relative to the displayed quote. Ondo Perps currently carries $23.5M in open interest across 26 pairs at a 0.07% average bid-ask spread, with OI up ~20x in the past month, positioning it as the tightest execution venue in the equity perps space. Its structural differentiator is using tokenized stocks as margin collateral rather than USDC, which improves capital efficiency but introduces compounding drawdown risk: a 20% haircut on $10k of NVDA collateral leaves $8k counting toward margin, and a simultaneous price drop erodes that buffer faster than a stablecoin position would. Backed by Ondo Global Markets' $18B in cumulative trading volume, 260+ tokenized US equities and ETFs, and ~50% tokenized stock market share by value ($858M per RWA.xyz), the platform is converting tokenization from a narrative into functioning derivatives infrastructure.

title: Vitalik on AI 2040 and the Pause Debate (5 minute read)
content: Ethereum founder Vitalik Buterin engaged with the AI 2040 policy paper, framing the debate as two incompatible worldviews: proponents assume superintelligence arrives by 2040 and warn of irreversible power concentration, while detractors assume the ASI transition goes smoothly by default without accounting for humanity's hard power dropping to zero. Buterin declined to join either camp but rejected the framing from major AI labs that positions one actor's "controlling global dominance" as the good outcome, calling it a trigger for every political alarm bell in any world resembling normal technology governance. He advocated for his d/acc platform, which emphasizes formal verification, cryptography, open hardware, and non-power-concentrating physical security, and noted that AI 2040 has improved by becoming more open-source friendly and incorporating "mutually assured compute destruction," giving multiple actors the ability to trigger a global compute winter rather than selectively disenfranchising others. As a legitimacy-building bridge, he proposed pre-agreed triggers (super-pandemics, unemployment exceeding 25%, and slaughterbots deployment) that, if enough fire within a set timeframe, would shift the community toward compute slowdowns, giving both accelerationists and pause advocates a reason to accept the framework.

title: Prediction Market or Manipulation Market? (5 minute read)
content: Research on Polymarket's five-minute Bitcoin contracts identifies a cross-market manipulation pattern where traders hold prediction market positions and place Binance spot orders in the final seconds before settlement, exploiting Chainlink's oracle dependence on Binance price data to influence resolution. A small cluster of accounts captured approximately $8.2 million in profits during periods classified as potentially manipulated, while ordinary participants absorbed about $7.6 million in losses. The structural flaw traces to two design choices: five-minute settlement windows create narrow but viable manipulation windows, and single-exchange oracle concentration amplifies the leverage a well-capitalized trader can apply at minimal cost relative to potential gains. The risk facing prediction markets more broadly is that information-discovery mechanisms become outcome-engineering venues when capital can move both the contract and the underlying reference price.

title: Thailand Investigating USDT for Money Laundering (1 minute read)
content: Thailand's Bank of Thailand and SEC are investigating USDT after finding that 40% of sellers on local platforms are non-residents.

title: Joseph Lubin argues Ethereum L1 fees should stay low to drive growth (2 minute read)
content: Robinhood Chain has generated just $1,538 in direct settlement revenue for Ethereum L1 despite grossing ~$816K overall.

title: American Bitcoin Plunges 95% From Peak (2 minute read)
content: American Bitcoin Corp, co-founded by Eric Trump, has shed over 95% of its peak value, erasing more than $600 million from Trump's roughly 6% stake.

title: Coinbase CEO admits Base "messed up" on content coins (1 minute read)
content: Brian Armstrong acknowledged Base's content coin initiatives like Zora and creator coins "didn't work," saying the platform already pivoted earlier this year toward trading, payments, and AI agents.

title: Housing bill including a CBDC ban passed without Trump's signature (4 minute read)
content: The 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act became law Friday without Trump's signature after he declined to sign in protest over unrelated voter-ID legislation, letting the 10-day constitutional default trigger enactment. The bipartisan bill, which passed 85-5 in the Senate and 358-32 in the House, bans the Federal Reserve from issuing a central bank digital currency. The CBDC ban has been a longstanding Republican priority and previously complicated passage of the GENIUS Act stablecoin law last year.

title: DOJ to Drop Charges Against $722M BitClub Network Fraud Mastermind (2 minute read)
content: The DOJ is preparing to drop charges with prejudice against Matthew Goettsche, the alleged architect of BitClub Network, a cryptocurrency mining fraud that prosecutors say defrauded investors of $722 million. Prosecutors alleged the scheme fabricated mining pool profit claims to attract capital while paying members to recruit others. Goettsche was indicted in 2019 on wire fraud and unregistered securities charges with a trial scheduled for October. The DOJ attributed the dismissal to the case's seven-year timeline and efforts to recover funds for victims, denying that pressure from defense attorneys influenced the decision. Three co-defendants previously pleaded guilty, and final dismissal terms remain under negotiation before submission to the court.

title: Ethlabs is online: Week 3 update (5 minute read)
content: Ethlabs posted a week-three update covering two closed hires, continued interop research including the Ethereum Economic Zone's synchronous composability work and the team's own async bridging lane built on the Open Intents Framework, and a push for faster Ethereum finality via a "quick slots" (EIP-8198) proposal ahead of the Hegotá upgrade's August 6 deadline. The team is tracking Robinhood Chain's rapid growth as a signal of L2 momentum and cited a new Cambridge study finding about 8,500 physical Ethereum nodes and 880,000 validators. Otherwise, Ethlabs remains in its fundraising phase, seeking one to two more anchor funders.

title: Bankr Agent Executes First Robinhood Crypto Trade via Tweet (2 minute read)
content: Bankr's rh-wallet skill executed what the team claims is the first AI agent-initiated crypto trade inside a live Robinhood account, triggered by tagging @bankrbot in a tweet with a plain-language instruction such as "buy $5 of SOL." The agent checks buying power and live prices across 30+ supported tokens, routes the order, and returns an on-chain receipt with no manual login, app, or browser interaction required. A demonstration trade of $1 DOGE filled instantly, and the team tokenized the milestone as $RHAGENT on Robinhood Chain as a commemorative and utility token. Planned additions include portfolio views and broader Robinhood account access, extending a social-to-settlement pipeline toward agentic execution of real financial actions across Base and Robinhood Chain.

title: Memecoins Are Back but for How Long? (5 minute read)
content: Robinhood Chain, launched July 1 for tokenized stocks and RWAs, saw memecoins become its dominant first-week use case, with a July 8 tweet from CEO Vlad Tenev endorsing the activity sending daily DEX volumes from $35M-$70M to $560M. The resurgence spans chains: Solana's $ANSEM went from zero to $400M market cap within days on Pump.fun before settling near $250M, while social trading app Fomo closed a $75M Series B at a $550M valuation and announced Robinhood Chain integration. There are structural infrastructure improvements (Apple Pay on-ramps, social leaderboards, multi-chain support) as an upgrade over prior cycles, but the underlying behavior remains speculative and narrative-driven. The durability question is whether these rails convert memecoin-attracted volume into retained wallets or RWA adoption, a conversion Solana managed but most chains have not.

title: Real Businesses Make the Ethereum Decision (7 minute read)
content: Crypto's next era favors Ethereum L1+L2 as real cash-flow businesses replace token-sale-driven projects in shaping infrastructure choices (e.g., Robinhood Chain and Coinbase's Base as evidence). Both use ETH as native gas and Ethereum for data availability and settlement despite being built by companies with no ideological allegiance to Ethereum. Ethereum's model is a "barbell," L1 for maximum decentralization and liquidity, L2s for customization and control. This shift is bullish for ETH as its role as collateral, liquidity, and monetary asset deepens across more products and users.

title: Culturally Crypto, Practically TradFi (6 minute read)
content: Robinhood Chain launched July 1 with 24/7 stock token trading across 120+ countries, integrating a Uniswap AMM, Morpho lending, and USDG as the settlement layer for the firm's 27.4 million funded accounts. The launch extends Robinhood's vertical integration playbook, which has absorbed Bitstamp ($200M), WonderFi, Lighter for perps, and Morpho for lending, collapsing TradFi's fragmented broker/exchange/custodian/clearing/issuer stack into a single composable system. With PFOF earning around 3 basis points, each owned layer raises Robinhood's take rate, and the chain serves as distribution leverage over 28 million users rather than a standalone revenue line. The logical next target is a proprietary stablecoin: USDG's $3.2B float, up from $1B in December, generates an estimated $119M/year in T-bill reserve income, a pool Coinbase harvested $305M from last quarter on a coin it does not issue.

title: This Week on Base: Beryl, B20, & More (3 minute read)
content: Base shipped Beryl and the B20 Native Token Standard to mainnet this week, and Aerodrome hit #1 in BTC-USD spot volume while capturing over 50% of onchain FX spot volume in Q2. Cloudflare opened an x402 monetization gateway for pages, datasets, APIs, and MCP tools, and Apify added 20,000+ web automation tools for agents via x402, widening the protocol's distribution across web infrastructure. Centrifuge deployed $50M+ in tokenized AAA CLOs on Base as Ethena collateral, and OpenStandard launched Open USD, a stablecoin for the internet economy, on Base. ClawBank's Manfred AI agent registered as the first zero-human US company, running a full agentic banking operation on Base.

title: Hedera Network Hit for $5.25M in Apparent Exploit (1 minute read)
content: An attacker exploited Hedera Mainnet for $5.25M and bridged the funds to Ethereum.

title: Robinhood Chain volume is mostly memecoin-driven (1 minute read)
content: The high DEX volume reported on Robinhood Chain is allegedly driven almost entirely by memecoin trading rather than other activity.

title: Michael Saylor posts cryptic Strategy chart after $216 million bitcoin sale (5 minute read)
content: Michael Saylor posted the company's bitcoin acquisition tracker with the ambiguous caption "Orange dots tell only part of the story," breaking from his usual purchase-signaling pattern days after Strategy sold 3,588 BTC for $216 million, its largest disposal ever.

title: ETH Distribution on Robinhood dashboard (1 minute read)
content: A new DefiLlama-built dashboard tracking ETH distribution on Robinhood Chain shows the L2 heavily uses and holds ETH.
